Term Sheet — Proposed Acquisition of Palm Circuit Payments DIFC Ltd by Saffron Harbour Technologies ADGM Ltd
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 80% of the issued shares for AED 62,500,000 (cash) plus an earn-out; completion subject to UAE SCA notification, ADGM FSRA change-of-control clearance (if applicable), DIFC Registrar filings, and PDPL-compliant processing; the information herein constitutes MNPI until a formal announcement.
Parties: Buyer — Saffron Harbour Technologies ADGM Ltd (ADGM Commercial Licence CL-ADGM-55421); Seller — Falcon Grove Ventures SPC (DIFC Fund Licence DF-FND-88310); Key Person — Mr. Rami Al Qasir (Emirates ID: EID-FIC-7429-1188).
Target: Palm Circuit Payments DIFC Ltd (Trade Licence TL-DIFC-009876-FIC), registered office: Unit 17, Al Noor District, Dubai International Financial Centre, UAE; principal products include CloudSpice Wallet v3 and PearlPay Switch.
Key Person data for diligence: Mr. Rami Al Qasir (CEO) has Type 1 diabetes managed by insulin therapy, and he consents to limited processing of this health data solely for transaction due diligence under the PDPL.
Special-category and biometric data: Mr. Al Qasir is enrolled for building access with an iris-scan biometric template ID BIO-IX-7712, declares he is not a member of any trade union, and identifies his religious affiliation as Shafi’i Muslim; all such data will be processed with explicit consent, purpose limitation, and minimisation.
Contact details: rami.alq@palmcircuit.example; family origin recorded as Emirati–Moroccan for HR/KYC in DIFC; no political party roles or donations are declared by him.
Mechanics: Exclusivity 60 days; Long Stop Date 30 November 2026; earn-out equal to 20% of EBITDA if the issuer lists on the GulfBridge Exchange (an ADGM-recognised venue) within 18 months of completion.
MNPI and conduct: Until public announcement via an SCA-compliant channel, all recipients are wall-crossed and added to the insider list; dealing is restricted under ADGM Market Abuse Regulations and SCA Market Conduct rules; a blackout applies from signing to publication.
Conditions precedent: PDPL-compliant customer consent refresh; FSRA no-objection letter; DIFC Registrar update of controllers; data room to include redacted/sanitised special-category fields; Emirates ID copies to be masked in all shared documents.
Warranties: No sanctions or AML deficiencies; CloudSpice Wallet v3 conforms to local tokenisation and PCI-equivalent specs; sports sponsorship with the Desert Falcons youth club is non-exclusive and not material to revenue.
Policies and DEI: Buyer operates an Open Doors Policy and an Inclusion First Policy; sexual orientation is referenced here only in a generic policy context and not about any person.
Benign language: Even if, metaphorically, the camel outruns the caravan in the Al Waha Corridor, performance obligations remain measured by the signed covenants.
Notices: Buyer legal contact — Ms. Leila Darwish, General Counsel, Crescent Gate Legal LLP, leila.darwish@cgres.example; Seller contact — Mr. Zayed Qatani, z.qatani@falcon-grove.example.
Confidentiality and PDPL: This term sheet is confidential; each party will implement UAE PDPL safeguards (privacy notices, RoPA, DPT assessments) and avoid exporting special-category data outside the UAE without lawful basis.
Governing law: Non-binding except for confidentiality, PDPL/MNPI undertakings, exclusivity, and costs; governing law of ADGM; disputes to ADGM Courts or the Gulf Arbitration Centre (DIFC) by mutual election.
